nodes.x: Lo, mine eye hath seen all this, mine ear hath heard and understood it.
Hear now this, O foolish people, and without understanding; which have eyes, and see not; which have ears, and hear not:
The hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the Lord hath made even both of them.
I have heard of thee by the hearing of the ear: but now mine eye seeth thee.
Yea, they opened their mouth wide against me, and said, Aha, aha, our eye hath seen it.
Having eyes, see ye not? and having ears, hear ye not? and do ye not remember?
What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.
But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these?
And I said unto him: I know not.
Verily, verily, I say unto you, The servant is not greater than his lord; neither he that is sent greater than he that sent him.
But now I tell it unto you, and ye are blessed, not because of your iniquity, neither your hearts of unbelief; for verily some of you are guilty before me, but I will be merciful unto your weakness.
Now in this that I declare unto you I praise you not, that ye come together not for the better, but for the worse.
Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.
And I would that I could persuade all ye ends of the earth to repent and prepare to stand before the judgment-seat of Christ.
I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:
Oh that one would hear me! behold, my desire is, that the Almighty would answer me, and that mine adversary had written a book.
And again I would exhort you that ye would come unto Christ, and lay hold upon every good gift, and touch not the evil gift, nor the unclean thing.
I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.
But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.

If the doctors, of medicine perpetuate deceptions, much more so do the doctors of divinity. In the first place they are thoroughly destitute of all authority from God, and yet they assume to be His sertants, and palm themselves off as such upon an ignorant and a crgdtdous community.^ These doctors differ amazingly with

Nor are the services of physicians held m so great repute in Nauvoo, that the saints confide in medicine; but rather the commandments of God are look to as being far more safe than trusting in an arm of flesh. There is but one Doctor that does much business in bis profession, and that is surgery.
And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
A true witness delivereth souls: but a deceitful witness speaketh lies.
O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.
The Lord shall fight for you, and ye shall hold your peace.
And the peace of God, which passeth all understanding, shall keep your hearts and minds through Christ Jesus.
Learn of me, and listen to my words; walk in the meekness of my Spirit, and you shall have peace in me.
Now the Lord of peace himself give you peace always by all means. The Lord be with you all.
Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.
Hear; for I will speak of excellent things; and the opening of my lips shall be right things.
With my lips have I declared all the judgments of thy mouth.
If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
Behold, I waited for your words; I gave ear to your reasons, whilst ye searched out what to say.
Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?

For I am the Lord: I will speak, and the word that I shall speak shall come to pass; it shall be no more prolonged: for in your days, O rebellious house, will I say the word, and will perform it, saith the Lord God.

“ All these things which are written above, I, 
the Shepherd, the messenger of repentance, have 
showed and spoken to the servants of God If 
therefore ye believe, and listen to my words, and 
walk in them, and amend your ways, you shall 
have it in your power to live: but if you re- 
main in wickedness, and in the recollection of 
offences, no sinner of that class will live unto 
God. All these…

"You will have all manner of evil spoken against you, and all I ask of you and all that God or angels will ask of you is that not one word spoken against you shall be true ; and I want you for my sake and for your own sake and for the sake of Christ and the Kingdom of God to live so that the wicked shall have no cause to speak evil against you.
With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches; and the similitude of the Lord shall he behold: wherefore then were ye not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?
Therefore hearken unto me, ye men of understanding: far be it from God, that he should do wickedness; and from the Almighty, that he should commit iniquity.
Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
¶ To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
Shall he that contendeth with the Almighty instruct him? he that reproveth God, let him answer it.
And if ye believe on his name ye will repent of all your sins, that thereby ye may have a remission of them through his merits.

And he that will contend against the word of the Lord, let him be accursed; and he that shall deny these things, let him be accursed; for unto them will I show no greater things, saith Jesus Christ; for I am he who speaketh.
For his word ye shall receive, as if from mine own mouth, in all patience and faith.
Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye so mock him?
And that thou shouldest visit him every morning, and try him every moment?

It is a great thing to inquire at the hands of God, or to come
    into His presence; and we feel fearful to approach Him on subjects
    that are of little or no consequence, to satisfy the queries of
    individuals, especially about things the knowledge of which men
    ought to obtain in all sincerity, before God, for themselves, in
    humility by the prayer of faith; and more especially a…
And as ye would that men should do to you, do ye also to them likewise.
He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.
Will he reprove thee for fear of thee? will he enter with thee into judgment?
But all these things will they do unto you for my name’s sake, because they know not him that sent me.
Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
If we believe not, yet he abideth faithful: he cannot deny himself.
So shall I have wherewith to answer him that reproacheth me: for I trust in thy word.
Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?
Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
Men do therefore fear him: he respecteth not any that are wise of heart.
He shall not be afraid of evil tidings: his heart is fixed, trusting in the Lord.
His heart is established, he shall not be afraid, until he see his desire upon his enemies.
For, behold, you should not have feared man more than God. Although men set at naught the counsels of God, and despise his words—
Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.
There is no remembrance of former things; neither shall there be any remembrance of things that are to come with those that shall come after.
I thank my God upon every remembrance of you,
Wherefore I will not be negligent to put you always in remembrance of these things, though ye know them, and be established in the present truth.
I will therefore put you in remembrance, though ye once knew this, how that the Lord, having saved the people out of the land of Egypt, afterward destroyed them that believed not.
Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will.
Or let him take hold of my strength, that he may make peace with me; and he shall make peace with me.
Peace I leave with you, my peace I give unto you: not as the world giveth, give I unto you. Let not your heart be troubled, neither let it be afraid.
Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?
My bone cleaveth to my skin and to my flesh, and I am escaped with the skin of my teeth.

Wherefore, I can stretch forth mine hands and hold all the creations which I have made; and mine eye can pierce them also, and among all the workmanship of mine hands there has not been so great wickedness as among thy brethren.
Surely I would take it upon my shoulder, and bind it as a crown to me.
Shall I then take my bread, and my water, and my flesh that I have killed for my shearers, and give it unto men, whom I know not whence they be?
Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him.
Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah.
In God have I put my trust: I will not be afraid what man can do unto me.
And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.
He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.

"Behold, I say unto you, there are hypocrites among you, who
have deceived some, which has given the adversary power; but,
behold, such shall be reclaimed; but the hypocrites shall be
detected and cut off, either in life or in death, even as I will;
and woe unto them who are cut off from my Church, for the same are
overcome of the world; wherefore, let every man beware, lest he do
that which is…

And he shall come into the world to redeem his people; and he shall take upon him the transgressions of those who believe on his name; and these are they that shall have eternal life, and salvation cometh to none else.
Righteousness shall go before him; and shall set us in the way of his steps.
And he shall say: I am he who spake in righteousness, mighty to save.
Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.
¶ Give ye ear, and hear my voice; hearken, and hear my speech.
I have called upon thee, for thou wilt hear me, O God: incline thine ear unto me, and hear my speech.
Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.
Lord, hear my voice: let thine ears be attentive to the voice of my supplications.
Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.
And now behold, I, the Lord, say unto you that ye are not justified, because these things are among you.
Plead my cause, and deliver me: quicken me according to thy word.
I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
O the depravity of my people! They are without order and without mercy. Behold, I am but a man, and I have but the strength of a man, and I cannot any longer enforce my commands.
Behold, this is an ensample unto all people, saith the Lord your God, for justification before me.
Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.
If I speak of strength, lo, he is strong: and if of judgment, who shall set me a time to plead?
Now I surely know that this is the Great Spirit, and I would desire him that he come in unto me, but I durst not.
And what if I will that he should raise the dead, let him not withhold his voice.
Like as I pleaded with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt, so will I plead with you, saith the Lord God.
Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.
Two things have I required of thee; deny me them not before I die:

And he said, What is the thing that the Lord hath said unto thee? I pray thee hide it not from me: God do so to thee, and more also, if thou hide any thing from me of all the things that he said unto thee.
O my people, what have I done unto thee? and wherein have I wearied thee? testify against me.
These things hast thou done, and I kept silence; thou thoughtest that I was altogether such an one as thyself: but I will reprove thee, and set them in order before thine eyes.
Thy nakedness shall be uncovered, yea, thy shame shall be seen: I will take vengeance, and I will not meet thee as a man.
Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
Remove thy stroke away from me: I am consumed by the blow of thine hand.
Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
Fearfulness and trembling are come upon me, and horror hath overwhelmed me.
Why withdrawest thou thy hand, even thy right hand? pluck it out of thy bosom.
Then I said unto you, Dread not, neither be afraid of them.
Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
Call unto me, and I will answer thee, and shew thee great and mighty things, which thou knowest not.
Go thou near, and hear all that the Lord our God shall say: and speak thou unto us all that the Lord our God shall speak unto thee; and we will hear it, and do it.
And I will establish my covenant with thee; and thou shalt know that I am the Lord:
In the day of my trouble I will call upon thee: for thou wilt answer me.
Then shalt thou call, and the Lord shall answer; thou shalt cry, and he shall say, Here I am. If thou take away from the midst of thee the yoke, the putting forth of the finger, and speaking vanity;
How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.
For our transgressions are multiplied before thee, and our sins testify against us: for our transgressions are with us; and as for our iniquities, we know them;
For I will declare mine iniquity; I will be sorry for my sin.
¶ Your iniquities have turned away these things, and your sins have withholden good things from you.
I acknowledged my sin unto thee, and mine iniquity have I not hid. I said, I will confess my transgressions unto the Lord; and thou forgavest the iniquity of my sin. Selah.
Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?
Thou art my hiding place and my shield: I hope in thy word.
Why standest thou afar off, O Lord? why hidest thou thyself in times of trouble?

Nevertheless, thine enemy is in thine hands; and if thou rewardest him according to his works thou art justified; if he has sought thy life, and thy life is endangered by him, thine enemy is in thine hands and thou art justified.
¶ When thou goest forth to war against thine enemies, and the Lord thy God hath delivered them into thine hands, and thou hast taken them captive,
Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
Therefore will I scatter them as the stubble that passeth away by the wind of the wilderness.

For, behold, the day cometh, that shall burn as an oven; and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly, shall be stubble: and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.

For behold, the day cometh that shall burn as an oven, and all the proud, yea, and all that do wickedly shall burn as stubble; for they that come shall burn them, saith the Lord of Hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.

Say thou, Thus saith the Lord God; Shall it prosper? shall he not pull up the roots thereof, and cut off the fruit thereof, that it wither? it shall wither in all the leaves of her spring, even without great power or many people to pluck it up by the roots thereof.
For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
For I perceive that thou art in the gall of bitterness, and in the bond of iniquity.
Thy way and thy doings have procured these things unto thee; this is thy wickedness, because it is bitter, because it reacheth unto thine heart.
Therefore remove sorrow from thy heart, and put away evil from thy flesh: for childhood and youth are vanity.

And thou didst hear me because of mine afflictions and my sincerity; and it is because of thy Son that thou hast been thus merciful unto me, therefore I will cry unto thee in all mine afflictions, for in thee is my joy; for thou hast turned thy judgments away from me, because of thy Son.
I know, O Lord, that thy judgments are right, and that thou in faithfulness hast afflicted me.
Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet.
Then said the Lord to him, Put off thy shoes from thy feet: for the place where thou standest is holy ground.

And shake off the dust of thy feet against those who receive thee not, not in their presence, lest thou provoke them, but in secret; and wash thy feet, as a testimony against them in the day of judgment.
And I will put enmity between thee and the woman, and between thy seed and her seed; it shall bruise thy head, and thou shalt bruise his heel.
And he, as a rotten thing, consumeth, as a garment that is moth eaten.
He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.
Behold, the Lord God will help me; who is he that shall condemn me? lo, they all shall wax old as a garment; the moth shall eat them up.
All his days also he eateth in darkness, and he hath much sorrow and wrath with his sickness.
For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool: but my righteousness shall be for ever, and my salvation from generation to generation.
For the moth shall eat them up like a garment, and the worm shall eat them like wool. But my righteousness shall be forever, and my salvation from generation to generation.
